fields.label: How did you find out someone used your identity?
fields.helpText: Pick the main reason you're filing this form. If more than one applies, pick the one that happened first.

options.label: The IRS sent me a letter or notice about it
I tried to file my taxes and they got rejected — someone already filed using my info
Social Security, a state agency, or another organization told me
I discovered it myself (credit report, strange mail, etc.)

fields.label: What was the IRS letter or notice number?
fields.helpText: Look at the top-right corner of the IRS letter. It should have a number. If you can't find it, just skip this.

fields.label: What did the thief do with your information?
fields.helpText: Pick the main thing that happened. If multiple things happened, pick the biggest one and describe the rest later.

options.label: Filed a fake tax return to steal my refund
Used my Social Security Number to get a job
Claimed me or my child as a dependent on their taxes

fields.label: Can you tell us more about what happened?
fields.placeholder: I noticed a W-2 from a company I never worked for...
fields.helpText: Any details help — when you noticed, what was wrong, anything unusual. This helps the IRS investigate.

fields.label: What tax year was affected?
fields.helpText: The most recent year when someone messed with your taxes. Usually last year.

fields.label: Were other years affected too?
fields.helpText: If this happened over multiple years, list them separated by commas. Leave blank if it was just one year.

fields.label: I wasn't even required to file taxes that year
fields.helpText: Check this if you didn't need to file a tax return for the year(s) listed above — for example, if you didn't work or earned too little.

fields.label: Do you know the name the thief used?
fields.helpText: If you know a different name appeared on the fake return, enter it here. Otherwise skip this.

fields.label: Now let's get your info — what is your first name?
fields.helpText: Your legal first name as it appears on your tax return.

fields.label: What is the SSN or ITIN that was misused?
fields.helpText: The 9-digit number that belongs to you — the one the thief used.

fields.label: What is your current street address?
fields.helpText: Where you currently live and get your mail.

fields.label: State? (2 letters)
fields.helpText: Two-letter state code, like TX, NY, CA, OK.

fields.label: ZIP+4? (optional)
fields.helpText: The extra 4 digits after your ZIP. Skip if you don't know.

fields.label: Did you recently move?
fields.helpText: If your address was different when the identity theft happened, we need that old address too.

fields.label: What was your old street address?
Home phone number? (optional)
fields.helpText: A landline number where the IRS can reach you.

fields.label: Cell phone number? (optional)
fields.helpText: A mobile number where the IRS can reach you.

fields.label: Best time to call? (optional)
fields.helpText: When should they try calling?

fields.label: What language do you prefer?
fields.helpText: The IRS will try to communicate with you in this language.

fields.label: Sign your name
fields.helpText: By signing, you swear under penalty of perjury that everything you wrote above is true. You can type your name or draw your signature.

fields.label: Who is filling out this form?
fields.helpText: If a lawyer, enrolled agent, parent, or guardian is doing this for you, pick the second option.

options.label: Someone else is doing it for me (attorney, EA, parent, etc.)

fields.label: What is this person to you?
Who was the court appointment for?
fields.helpText: The court may have appointed this person for a specific reason.

options.label: As my parent or legal guardian

fields.label: Describe the court appointment
fields.placeholder: Conservator, legal guardian of estate, etc.

fields.label: Court has accepted responsibility for this person's affairs
fields.helpText: Check this if the court order specifically grants authority to manage tax matters.

fields.label: Describe who they are
fields.placeholder: Enrolled Agent, Tax Preparer, CPA, etc.

fields.label: Their CAF number? (optional)
fields.helpText: Centralized Authorization File number. Your tax preparer or attorney will know this. Skip if they don't have one.
